title: Intensely Sweet Japanese Sweet Potato Mochi
description: A traditional Japanese dish featuring sweet potato mochi, this recipe is kid-friendly and paleo-friendly, with a jammy and crumbly texture.
instructions: Preheat the binchotan grill to medium heat.
In a large bowl, combine mochi flour, coconut sugar, and salt.
Gradually add in water and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and shape into small balls.
Flatten each ball into a small disk shape.
Brush the katsuramuki-cut sweet potatoes with ground nut oil and season with vanilla extract.
Place a sweet potato slice in the center of each mochi disk.
Fold the mochi over the sweet potato to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Grill the mochi for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until lightly charred and cooked through.
